kwargs.content:
I need you to process the meter readings for tour J09B. Take all entries from the decision_table where the TOUR starts with "J09B" and put them into a temporary table called J09B_temp. Then add a new column called ENTSCHEIDUNG to that table.

For classifying each house: count how many equipment numbers (EQUNR) belong to each house (HAUS). If a house has fewer than 3 equipment numbers, set its ENTSCHEIDUNG to "KSA". Otherwise, set it to "EVU". There are two additional overrides: any house where HOUSE_NUM1 is 0 should be set to "EVU", and any house where HOUSE_NUM1 is in the range 500 to 599 should also be set to "EVU".

Next, enrich the data by joining it with the EABLG table using the INSTALLATION field, and also pull in customer contact details from both the net_master and sales_master tables using the EQUNR field. When both net and sales data exist for a customer, prefer the sales data and leave the net data fields empty. If only net data exists, keep that.

Export the final result as a CSV file named "J09B" with these columns: `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`.

Send the CSV to the EVU team at meter.readings@evu.com. After that, delete the temporary table.

args.sqlite_command:
CREATE TABLE J09B_temp AS
SELECT d.*, NULL AS ENTSCHEIDUNG
FROM decision_table d
WHERE d.TOUR LIKE 'J09B%';

UPDATE J09B_temp
SET ENTSCHEIDUNG = CASE
    WHEN HOUSE_NUM1 = 0 THEN 'EVU'
    WHEN HOUSE_NUM1 BETWEEN 500 AND 599 THEN 'EVU'
    ELSE (
        CASE
            WHEN (
                SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT EQUNR)
                FROM J09B_temp t2
                WHERE t2.HAUS = J09B_temp.HAUS
            ) < 3 THEN 'KSA'
            ELSE 'EVU'
        END
    )
;

UPDATE J09B_temp
SET ENTSCHEIDUNG = CASE
    WHEN HOUSE_NUM1 = 0 THEN 'EVU'
    WHEN HOUSE_NUM1 BETWEEN 500 AND 599 THEN 'EVU'
    ELSE (
        CASE
            WHEN (
                SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT EQUNR)
                FROM J09B_temp t2
                WHERE t2.HAUS = J09B_temp.HAUS
            ) < 3 THEN 'KSA'
            ELSE 'EVU'
        END
    )
END;

args.sql_query:
WITH base AS (
    SELECT 
        j."Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
        j.HAUS,
        j.ANLAGE,
        j.ME_MA_ID,
        j.EQUNR,
        j.HOUSE_NUM1,
        j.ENTSCHEIDUNG,
        COALESCE(s.TITLE, n.TITLE) AS TITLE,
        COALESCE(s.FORENAME, n.FORENAME) AS FORENAME,
        COALESCE(s.SURNAME, n.SURNAME) AS SURNAME,
        COALESCE(s.STREET, n.STREET) AS STREET,
        COALESCE(s.STREETNO, n.STREETNO) AS STREETNO,
        COALESCE(s.POST_CODE1, n.POST_CODE1) AS POST_CODE1,
        COALESCE(s.CITY1, n.CITY1) AS CITY1,
        COALESCE(s.CITY2, n.CITY2) AS CITY2,
        COALESCE(s.EMAIL, n.EMAIL) AS EMAIL
    FROM J09B_temp j
    LEFT JOIN eablg e ON e.Installation = j.ANLAGE
    LEFT JOIN net_master n ON n.EQUNR = j.EQUNR
    LEFT JOIN sales_master s ON s.EQUNR = j.EQUNR
)
SELECT 
    "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
    HAUS,
    ANLAGE,
    ME_MA_ID,
    EQUNR,
    HOUSE_NUM1,
    ENTSCHEIDUNG,
    TITLE,
    FORENAME,
    SURNAME,
    STREET,
    STREETNO,
    POST_CODE1,
    CITY1,
    CITY2,
    EMAIL
FROM base;

args.sql_query:
WITH base AS (
    SELECT 
        e."Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No." AS "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
        j.HAUS,
        j.ANLAGE,
        j.ME_MA_ID,
        j.EQUNR,
        j.HOUSE_NUM1,
        j.ENTSCHEIDUNG,
        CASE WHEN s.EQUNR IS NOT NULL THEN s.TITLE ELSE n.TITLE END AS TITLE,
        CASE WHEN s.EQUNR IS NOT NULL THEN s.FORENAME ELSE n.FORENAME END AS FORENAME,
        CASE WHEN s.EQUNR IS NOT NULL THEN s.SURNAME ELSE n.SURNAME END AS SURNAME,
        CASE WHEN s.EQUNR IS NOT NULL THEN s.STREET ELSE n.STREET END AS STREET,
        CASE WHEN s.EQUNR IS NOT NULL THEN s.STREETNO ELSE n.STREETNO END AS STREETNO,
        CASE WHEN s.EQUNR IS NOT NULL THEN s.POST_CODE1 ELSE n.POST_CODE1 END AS POST_CODE1,
        CASE WHEN s.EQUNR IS NOT NULL THEN s.CITY1 ELSE n.CITY1 END AS CITY1,
        CASE WHEN s.EQUNR IS NOT NULL THEN s.CITY2 ELSE n.CITY2 END AS CITY2,
        CASE WHEN s.EQUNR IS NOT NULL THEN s.EMAIL ELSE n.EMAIL END AS EMAIL
    FROM J09B_temp j
    LEFT JOIN eablg e ON e.Installation = j.ANLAGE
    LEFT JOIN net_master n ON n.EQUNR = j.EQUNR
    LEFT JOIN sales_master s ON s.EQUNR = j.EQUNR
)
SELECT 
    "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
    HAUS,
    ANLAGE,
    ME_MA_ID,
    EQUNR,
    HOUSE_NUM1,
    ENTSCHEIDUNG,
    TITLE,
    FORENAME,
    SURNAME,
    STREET,
    STREETNO,
    POST_CODE1,
    CITY1,
    CITY2,
    EMAIL
FROM base;

args.email:
Hello EVU team,

Please find attached the processed meter reading data for tour J09B.

Best regards,
Meter Reading Automation

kwargs.content:
I’ve completed the requested process:

1. Created a temporary table `J09B_temp` containing all rows from `decision_table` where `TOUR` starts with `J09B`.
2. Added the `ENTSCHEIDUNG` column and populated it using your rules:
   - If `HOUSE_NUM1 = 0` → `ENTSCHEIDUNG = 'EVU'`
   - If `HOUSE_NUM1` between 500 and 599 → `ENTSCHEIDUNG = 'EVU'`
   - Otherwise, if the house (`HAUS`) has fewer than 3 distinct `EQUNR` → `ENTSCHEIDUNG = 'KSA'`
   - All other cases → `ENTSCHEIDUNG = 'EVU'`
3. Enriched the data:
   - Joined with `eablg` via `ANLAGE` = `Installation` to obtain `"Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No."`.
   - Joined with `net_master` and `sales_master` via `EQUNR`.
   - When both net and sales records existed, I used the sales fields and did not use the net fields; when only net existed, I kept the net data.
4. Exported the final result to a CSV named `J09B.csv` with exactly these columns, in this order:  
   `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`.  
   - 20,180 rows were exported.
5. Sent the CSV file `J09B.csv` to the EVU team at `meter.readings@evu.com`.
6. Dropped the temporary table `J09B_temp` from the database.

If you’d like, I can also produce a quick summary (e.g., how many houses were classified as `KSA` vs `EVU`).
